market_review.diagnosis: The live read correctly identified starter benching risk, but underestimated fourth-quarter bench scoring. Philadelphia scored 31 in Q4 and cleared 108.5.
The rebound ladder failed because Embiid had only five rebounds through three and played 0:00 in Q4 after the blowout reached full bench conditions.

session_result.bankroll_state_note: User reported bankroll at zero after settlement.

critical_game_flow: NYK led 43-24 after the first quarter behind extreme three-point shooting.
NYK led 81-57 at halftime; PHI remained live for team-total markets because 57 points left a reachable fourth-quarter path.
NYK led 122-83 after three. PHI needed 26+ Q4 points to clear 108.5 and scored 31.
Q4 was played almost entirely by bench units. Embiid, Maxey, George and Oubre recorded 0:00 in Q4, but PHI bench scoring still beat the team-total threshold.

what_model_got_right: Knicks control and sweep pressure were correctly identified.
Embiid rebound over became a dead-zone after 3Q.
Starter benching in Q4 was correctly anticipated.
what_model_missed: Philadelphia bench scoring tail was materially higher than projected.
Starter benching was incorrectly translated into team-total suppression.
The live NO 108.5 position lacked enough Q4 buffer.

recommended_patches.action: Add a fourth-quarter bench scoring tail when a team has 80+ through three even if trailing by 25+.
Reject NO team-total positions unless model median is at least six points below the live line when Q4 scoring below 26 is required.
Separate starter-prop kill switches from team-total projections and run a bench-only total model.
Move to cooldown and paper-tracking mode before the next live entry cycle.

rolling_window_notes_for_future: Game 4 confirms blowout team-total unders remain dangerous if the losing team already has 80+ through three.
Starter benching is useful for player-prop fades but not sufficient for team-total unders.
For future NYK games, opposing bench scoring tail must be modeled separately once New York creates a 25+ point lead.
